parameters.name: smpte
parameters.label: SMPTE
parameters.description: When enabled the generated timecode will follow the standard, meaning no negative timecodes, and the timecode will loop at 24 hours. When disabled, the format will be more general, allowing for negati

parameters.name: mode
parameters.label: Mode
parameters.description: The source used for generating the timecode

parameters.name: rate
parameters.label: Rate
parameters.description: The timecode . The timecode's max frame value is equal to . If a fractional rate is provided then the rate is rounded up to the nearest whole number and drop-frames will be calculated if enabled on th

parameters.name: dropframe
parameters.label: Drop Frame
parameters.description: Specify how to calculate . Drop frames are used when the is fractional. cannot increment a fractional amount per frame so is rounded to the next whole number and the accumulation of error is accommoda

parameters.name: index
parameters.label: Index
parameters.description: The index used to generate the timecode. Value is used in conjunction with the specified units.

parameters.name: indexunit
parameters.label: Index Unit
parameters.description: The index value units.

parameters.name: timecodestr
parameters.label: Timecode String
parameters.description: A timecode string following the format: or

parameters.name: frame
parameters.label: Frame
parameters.description: Frame component of the timecode.

parameters.name: second
parameters.label: Second
parameters.description: Second component of the timecode.

parameters.name: minute
parameters.label: Minute
parameters.description: Minute component of the timecode. Allows for overflow.

parameters.name: hour
parameters.label: Hour
parameters.description: Hour component of the timecode.

parameters.name: init
parameters.label: Initialize
parameters.description: Used in sequential mode. Initializes the timecode value.

parameters.name: start
parameters.label: Start
parameters.description: Used in sequential mode. Starts the timecode sequential increment.

parameters.name: play
parameters.label: Play
parameters.description: When enabled, the sequential timecode will step forward.

parameters.name: timecodeobj
parameters.label: Timecode Object
parameters.description: A timecode object.

parameters.name: chop
parameters.label: CHOP
parameters.description: A reference which contains some or all of the following channels: negative, hour, minute, second, frame.

parameters.name: op
parameters.label: OP
parameters.description: An OP reference that contains a timecode Python member: eg. .

parameters.name: customlength
parameters.label: Custom Length
parameters.description: If set to "By Value", then the Length parameter is used. If set to "By Timecode", then the Length Timecode Object is used. If set to "Off", then the default length will be 23:59:59:ff-1 for a SMPTE ti

parameters.name: length
parameters.label: Length
parameters.description: Specifies the custom length in either samples, frames, or seconds.

parameters.name: lengthunits
parameters.label: Length Units
parameters.description: The unit of the custom length.

parameters.name: negativechan
parameters.label: Negative Chan
parameters.description: When enabled, outputs the negative channel, which is true when the timecode is negative. Always false when using SMPTE standard.

parameters.name: framechan
parameters.label: Frame Chan
parameters.description: When enabled, outputs the frame channel.

parameters.name: secondchan
parameters.label: Second Chan
parameters.description: When enabled, outputs the second channel.

parameters.name: minutechan
parameters.label: Minute Chan
parameters.description: When enabled, outputs the minute channel.

parameters.name: hourchan
parameters.label: Hour Chan
parameters.description: When enabled, outputs the hour channel.

parameters.name: totalseconds
parameters.label: Total Seconds Chan
parameters.description: When enabled, outputs the total_seconds channel, which is the timecode converted into seconds.

parameters.name: totalframes
parameters.label: Total Frames Chan
parameters.description: When enabled, outputs the total_frames channel, which is the timecode into frames.

parameters.name: dropframechan
parameters.label: Drop Frame Chan
parameters.description: When enabled, outputs the drop_frame channel, which is true when the timecode is drop-frame.

parameters.name: fpschan
parameters.label: FPS Chan
parameters.description: When enabled, outputs the fps channel.

parameters.name: timeslice
parameters.label: Time Slice
parameters.description: Turning this on forces the channels to be " ".  A is the time between the last cook frame and the current cook frame.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.label: Scope
parameters.description: To determine which channels get affected, some CHOPs use a string on the Common page.

parameters.name: srselect
parameters.label: Sample Rate Match
parameters.description: Handle cases where multiple input CHOPs' sample rates are different. When Resampling occurs, the curves are interpolated according to the Interpolation Method Option, or "Linear" if the Interpolate Op

parameters.name: exportmethod
parameters.label: Export Method
parameters.description: This will determine how to connect the channel to the parameter. Refer to the article for more information.

parameters.name: autoexportroot
parameters.label: Export Root
parameters.description: This path points to the root node where all of the paths that exporting by are relative to.

parameters.name: exporttable
parameters.label: Export Table
parameters.description: The used to hold the export information when using the Table Methods (See above).

parameters.name: countdown
parameters.label: Countdown
parameters.description: Help Not Available.

parameters.name: cycle
parameters.label: Cycle
parameters.description: When enabled, the timecode value will cycle back to 00:00:00:00 upon reaching the custom length, rather than holding the last value.
